---
title: "Extended operational checklist"
subtitle: "For building a study, not for reporting one."
toc: true
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**This is not the paper's checklist.** The paper's instrument is the
ten-item [Minimum Reporting Checklist](index.qmd), which specifies what a study
should *disclose* so a reader can assess it.

This longer list is an operational companion covering what a team should *do*,
stage by stage, so that the MRC can be answered honestly at the end. Most of
these decisions never appear in a methods section. It is offered as practice,
not as a reporting standard, and carries no claim to completeness.
:::

Each item maps to the MRC item it ultimately serves, or to a taxonomy row it
guards against.

```{python}
#| echo: false
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from IPython.display import Markdown

spec = yaml.safe_load(Path("checklist/acquire-checklist.yml").read_text())

lines = [f"**Revised {spec['revised']}** · {len(spec['items'])} items · "
         "not part of the cited specification\n",
         "\n**Study types used for filtering:**\n"]
for key, desc in spec["study_types"].items():
    lines.append(f"- `{key}` — {desc}")
Markdown("\n".join(lines))
```

## Items by stage

```{python}
#| echo: false
STAGE_ORDER = ["design", "instrument", "pilot", "deploy", "monitor", "curate", "release"]
by_stage = {}
for item in spec["items"]:
    by_stage.setdefault(item["stage"], []).append(item)

out = []
for stage in STAGE_ORDER:
    items = by_stage.get(stage, [])
    if not items:
        continue
    out.append(f"### {stage.capitalize()}\n")
    out.append(f"[{len(items)} items]{{.acq-label}}\n")
    out.append("| | Statement | Applies to | Guards against |")
    out.append("|:--|:--|:--|:--|")
    for item in items:
        applies = ", ".join(f"`{t}`" for t in item.get("applies_when", []))
        recipe = item.get("recipe")
        link = f"[detail](../{recipe}.qmd)" if recipe else ""
        out.append(
            f"| `{item['id']}` | {item['statement'].strip()} | {applies} | {link} |"
        )
    out.append("")
Markdown("\n".join(out))
```

## How this relates to the reporting checklist

The two instruments answer different questions and are used at different times.

| | Minimum Reporting Checklist | Extended operational checklist |
|:--|:--|:--|
| **Question** | What must a reader be told? | What should a team do? |
| **Used** | When writing the methods section | Throughout the study |
| **Audience** | Authors, reviewers, curators | Engineers, PhD students, PIs |
| **Status** | The paper's instrument, v26.7 | Practice, no standing as a standard |
| **Length** | 10 items | 65 items |

A study can complete the extended checklist and still report nothing; a study
can report everything on the MRC without having done any of this. The intended
relationship is that doing the first makes the second answerable with real
numbers instead of silence.

## Source

[`checklist/acquire-checklist.yml`](https://github.com/acquire-framework/acquire-framework.github.io/blob/main/checklist/acquire-checklist.yml).

Items here are less settled than the MRC and were not derived by the procedure
described in the paper. Disagreement is expected and welcome.
